business.headline: Asian markets crash, will Dalal Street feel the aftershock on Monday?From South Korea's Kospi to Japan's Nikkei, Asian stock markets have tumbled sharply, leaving investors on edge. As Dalal Street prepares to reopen after a three-day holiday on Monday, will the BSE and NSE also open deep in the red?

From ITR filing to TDS: 3 major tax deadlines in July 2026 you shouldn't missWhether you're a salaried employee, a business owner or an employer, keeping track of these dates can help you avoid last-minute stress. Here are three key tax deadlines you should not miss in July 2026.

Will your family get your EPF pension after your death? Here's what you need to knowNo one likes to think about what happens after they're gone. But if you're an EPF member, knowing whether your family can continue receiving your pension could make a big difference to their financial future.

Will DA rise before the 8th Pay Commission? Here's what the latest data suggestsThe wait for the 8th Pay Commission continues, but all eyes are now on the next DA revision. As inflation remains elevated and the July review draws closer, is another DA hike just around the corner?

Govt restores commercial LPG supply to pre-West Asia crisis levelsThe move comes after the government reviewed the country's fuel availability and found that both domestic production and imported LPG supplies have improved.

AI reality check hits Asia as Seoul's Kospi crashes 8%, Tokyo's Nikkei tumbles 5%South Korea's KOSPI fell 8.2%, prompting authorities to halt program trading for 20 minutes after steep losses rippled across the market. Japan's Nikkei 225 dropped 5%, while Hong Kong's Hang Seng declined 2.4%.

What's the one big hurdle delaying India-US trade deal? Piyush Goyal explainsPiyush Goyal said India will bring the US trade deal into force only after Washington creates a tariff framework that favours Indian exporters over regional competitors. The position follows US legal changes that overturned reciprocal tariffs and forced both sides to revisit implementation.

Sebi may allow celebrity endorsements for financial brands. But here's a catchUnder the proposal, celebrities would be allowed to endorse a financial firm's brand or company name, subject to prescribed conditions, disclaimers and prior approvals.

Stock market holiday on Muharram: Are NSE and BSE open for trading on June 26?Planning to trade on Friday, June 26? With Muharram being observed across the country, many investors are wondering whether the NSE and BSE will remain open for trading?

Don't want to pay taxes online? Here's how to use the 'Pay at Bank Counter' optionNot comfortable paying taxes online? You're not alone. The Income Tax Department still allows taxpayers to pay their dues at authorised bank branches through the 'Pay at Bank Counter' facility.

India-US trade deal very close, but here's why it isn't signed yetPiyush Goyal said India and the US are close to finalising their trade deal. India wants tariff terms that give its exporters a clear edge over rivals.

Dalal Street pares gains: Sensex ends flat after climbing over 600 pointsEarly optimism driven by lower crude oil prices and a stronger rupee faded as investors booked profits, though benchmark indices still managed to end in positive territory.

Zerodha's Nithin Kamath has a warning for you against pyramid schemesAccording to figures cited by Kamath, more than 5.5 crore Indians have lost their savings to over 5,300 such schemes. The estimated losses stood at around Rs 10 lakh crore as of 2015, and he suggested that the actual figure could be much higher now.

Gold loses over Rs 51,000 from all-time high. Why is the yellow metal crashing?Gold has fallen sharply from its peak as silver also comes under selling pressure. The correction is being driven by rate-hike bets, a stronger dollar and shifting investor appetite.

United Spirits may cut 100 jobs as Diageo looks to trim costsUnited Spirits is set to lay off around 100 employees as Diageo pursues a global cost-cutting programme. The move comes amid a wider restructuring drive in India despite strong growth and continued investment in premium brands.

Amazon to invest $48 billion in India by 2030, CEO Andy Jassy meets PM ModiAccording to Jassy, the company aims to support 3.8 million jobs in India by 2030 and help generate $80 billion in cumulative e-commerce exports.

Sensex jumps over 600 points: Why are stock markets rallying today?Benchmark indices extended their recovery on Thursday as softer crude, firm Asian cues and gains in banks and autos lifted sentiment. The move has eased near-term macro concerns, though investors remain watchful of the monsoon and global central bank signals.

EPFO portal shutdown from June 26-28: How it will affect your EPF claimsPlanning to withdraw money from your EPF account or submit a claim this week? You may have to postpone it. EPFO has announced a three-day shutdown of key portal services as it upgrades its claims processing system.

GTA 5 made $10 billion. Here's what GTA 6 could earn if it repeats historyGrand Theft Auto V is the second best-selling video game ever made. As pre-orders open for its successor, here is what the numbers say about how big GTA 6 could really be.

7 reasons why you must file ITR even if your income falls below exemption limitThink your income is too low to require an ITR? Not necessarily. Certain financial transactions can make return filing mandatory even if your income is below the exemption limit.

entertainment.headline: Akshay Kumar says Anil Kapoor-Nana Patekar are family, hints at Welcome 4 return Actor Akshay Kumar said actors Anil Kapoor and Nana Patekar remain part of the Welcome family despite missing Welcome To The Jungle. His remarks keep the door open for a reunion in a fourth film.

Rahul Ravindran defends Rashmika's The Girlfriend amid Pune murder case row Director Rahul Ravindran responded after social media users linked The Girlfriend to the Ketan Agarwal murder case. His post drew a distinction between individual crimes and the wider gender imbalance his film addresses.

Inside Khushbu Sundar's daughter's wedding with bridesmaids Trisha, Tamannaah Actor Tamannaah Bhatia shared glimpses from actor-politician Khushbu Sundar's daughter Avantika Sundar's star-studded wedding in Goa. The dreamy photos feature candid moments with Trisha Krishnan and Aarti Ravi.

Fast & Furious star Tyrese Gibson lands in Jamnagar as Ambani family guest Actor Tyrese Gibson arrived in Jamnagar after receiving a special invitation from the Ambani family. His airport appearance, marked by waves, smiles and a brief dance, drew local attention.

Actor Vishnu Vishal defends Rajinikanth, says how can he be jealous of CM Vijay? Actor Vishnu Vishal expressed his pain over superstar Rajinikanth having to publicly deny jealousy towards Tamil Nadu CM Vijay amidst online controversies. He highlighted the harsh scrutiny celebrities face on social media and urged fans not to confuse professional respect with personal rivalry.

Alliance Ep 1 review: Kushal-Zaid's history, Ravi Kishan's charm in a deja vu showRelease Date:Fri Jun 26, 2026 Prime Video's Alliance opens with 16 contestants entering Kunal Lok and forming early equations. Actor Kunal Kemmu stands out as host, but the premiere saves its only real spark for a late risk.

100 days of Dhurandhar 2: Rs 1,813 crore box office, all for Honsla Eendhan Badla Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge has completed 100 days in theatres, continuing its blockbuster run. The Aditya Dhar directorial has grossed Rs 1,813.38 crore worldwide, emerging as one of the biggest hits of recent years.

Half Man: Richard Gadd's show on toxic masculinity set for India streaming debut Actors Richard Gadd and Jamie Bell lead Half Man, a six-episode drama is set for its India streaming debut on July 3. The series traces a decades-long bond to examine masculinity, trauma, violence and emotional silence.

Mirzapur The Movie unveils Jitendra, Ravi Kishan, Abhishek Banerjee's intense looks The makers of Mirzapur: The Movie have unveiled new posters featuring Jitendra Kumar, Ravi Kishan and Abhishek Banerjee. The campaign broadens the film's character focus ahead of its 4 September 2026 theatrical release.
Why Kunal Kemmu took his first reality TV hosting gig with Alliance? Kunal Kemmu has stepped into reality television as the host of Prime Video's Alliance. The show premiered on June 26.

Prabhas recalls most stressful phase after Baahubali: I couldn't sleep In Baahubali: The Torchbearer, Prabhas said the film's success left him under intense pressure after release. He said that pressure shaped his choices and paved the way for Salaar and Kalki 2898 AD.

How many actors are in Welcome To The Jungle? Ahmed Khan reveals the count Director Ahmed Khan revealed the massive cast of Akshay Kumar-led comedy Welcome To The Jungle. He compared working with the crew to managing a daily music concert.

No Bollywood comedy can top Welcome. Bolne de... bechare ko takleef hua hai As Welcome to the Jungle gears up for release, it's impossible not to look back at Welcome – Bollywood's happiest accident of chaos. Nearly 20 years later, its dialogues are still quoted, its scenes are still meme material, and Majnu Bhai is still very much an artist.

Is Rajinikanth's Dharman a remake of The Good Doctor? Director breaks silence Director Ashwath Marimuthu has addressed social media claims that Rajinikanth's Dharman is an adaptation of The Good Doctor. The clarification comes as the film's doctor-themed tagline and medical setting continue to fuel online speculation.

Raja Shivaji on OTT: When and where to watch Riteish Deshmukh's historical-drama Riteish Deshmukh's Raja Shivaji has started streaming on Netflix in Marathi and Hindi. The OTT release extends the reach of the record-breaking Marathi hit beyond its theatrical run.

Kangana Ranaut says families should not be judged in Pune fort murder case Actor Kangana Ranaut has voiced that families should not be blamed for the actions of their children in the Pune fort murder case, emphasising the influence of social media, AI, and peer pressure.

Woman tells SRK, 'I love you more than my husband'; his reply is pure king energy Shah Rukh Khan left fans in splits after jokingly responding to a woman who said she loved him more than her husband during an event in Mangaluru. The video has since gone viral on social media.

50 vanity vans, 900 people: Ahmed Khan breaks down the scale of Welcome 3 shoot Director Ahmed Khan has opened up about the scale of mounting Welcome To The Jungle with 34 actors and hundreds on set. The film hit theatres on June 26.

Heartin review: Sananth-Madonna Sebastian's love triangle never finds its sparkRelease Date:Fri Jun 26, 2026 Heartin movie review: Director Kishore Kumar's Heartin, starring Sananth, Madonna Sebastian and Emaya, is a romantic drama that explores modern relationships and second chances. The film took a formulaic approach and could have benefited from better performances from its women.

general news.headline: 2,004 calls, 48-minute trek, failed alibi: How Ketan's murder plot unravelledFrom secret cafe meetings and 2,004 phone calls to a failed alibi and conflicting claims, Ketan Agarwal's murder has uncovered a meticulous conspiracy by Siya Goyal and her boyfriend Chetan Chaudhary.

Ireland ruin Shreyas Iyer's captaincy debut to seal historic T20I winIRE vs IND, 1st T20I: Lorcan Tucker's Ireland stunned Shreyas Iyer's India on Friday, beating the reigning T20 world champions by 34 runs in Belfast. Chasing 183 for victory, India were bowled out for 148, as Ireland registered their first-ever win over India in international cricket.

Foolish violation of ceasefire: Trump says Iran struck 4 drones at ships in HormuzUS President Donald Trump accused Iran of violating the ceasefire by launching four attack drones at commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz, claiming one cargo vessel was hit while US forces intercepted the other three.
US, Israel, Lebanon sign trilateral framework agreement after 4 days of talks in WashingtonUS, Israel, Lebanon sign trilateral framework agreement after 4 days of talks in Washington

Saayoni Ghosh is not a paltu, voters are OG paltuThe voters of Saayoni Ghosh's Jadavpur constituency flipped before she did. They are the original paltus. And, what does a true representative of the people do if not swing with the voters' mood?

Iran shook US Gulf fortress. The scale of war damage is finally coming into focusRepeated Iranian missile and drone strikes hit key facilities at the US naval base in Bahrain. The damage has revived Pentagon plans to reshape its Gulf footprint against newer threats.

Jana Nayagan producer appointed Tamil Nadu Special Representative to DelhiTamil Nadu has appointed K Venkata Narayana as its Special Representative in New Delhi for one year. The move has drawn opposition criticism over his film industry background and proximity to C Joseph Vijay.

India FTA terms changed covertly to block immigrants, says New Zealand ministerNew Zealand's ruling party has covertly changed the terms of the Free Trade Agreement (FTA) signed with India to prevent the immigration of Indian students and workers, alleged Winston Peters, the foreign minister and head of New Zealand First. Peters's party is part of the coalition headed by the National Party, which denied his claims.

13-year-old creates history, becomes first female heir to Rajasthan royal familyThe ceremony took place at the historic Kherwa Fort, believed to date back to the 17th century, and was attended by hundreds of villagers. It marked the first time in the region that a girl was recognised as an heir under this customary Rajput succession practice.

Siya, her boyfriend deleted chats, recycle bin before and after killing Ketan: CopsPolice said Siya Goyal and Chetan Chaudhary deleted chats before and after Ketan Agarwal's death at Lohagad Fort. The claim surfaced as investigators sent both phones for forensic recovery and the state approved a fast-track trial.

Fresh setback for Tejas Mk1A programme as sixth US engine develops snagThe development comes at a time when the Tejas Mk1A programme is already running more than two years behind schedule, raising concerns within the Ministry of Defence and the Indian Air Force (IAF). Sources told India Today that the seventh F404-IN20 engine is expected to be delivered to HAL next month.

Shashi Tharoor wades into passport-citizenship debate. Here's his solutionThe debate gained momentum after the Ministry of External Affairs clarified that while the passport is issued exclusively to Indian citizens, it does not in itself establish citizenship. The clarification, which came on the 14th Passport Seva Divas on June 24, sparked wider discussion on what constitutes conclusive proof of nationality.

Where is monsoon? Satellite captures rain clouds missing over IndiaSatellite imagery shows large parts of India without the deep rain clouds expected in late June despite the monsoon's advance. Forecasters say a developing system over the eastern Indian Ocean could revive rainfall, though its timing remains uncertain.

Why 35°C in Britain feels unbearable than 45°C heatwave in IndiaBritain's heatwave has revived comparisons with India's far higher summer temperatures. Experts say humidity, heat-trapping homes and poor acclimatisation make lower temperatures feel harsher.

Ketan wore hair patch, Siya knew of it: Father amid swirling murder theoriesKetan Agarwal's use of a wig has emerged as a fresh angle in the Pune murder case, though police say it alone cannot explain the motive behind Siya's decision to kill him.

Ram Mandir trust chief Champat Rai quits day after arrests in donation theft caseThe resignations follow growing scrutiny after the SIT's preliminary report reportedly identified major lapses in managing and monitoring donations made by devotees at the Ram Temple.

Hitler killed Jews; Jews used Hitler's weapons to defeat ArabsOne of history's greatest ironies unfolded in 1948. Weapons originally designed for Hitler's Nazi Germany, which killed nearly 6 million Jews, ended up helping defend the newly formed Jewish state of Israel. Jews sourced Nazi-era weapons and planes from Czechoslovakia, and beat back the armies of five Arab states.

Satan took over me: Afghan man's bizarre defence for raping 2 teens at his UK flatAn Afghan asylum seeker on trial in Bolton said in court that Satan took over after he drank alcohol. The claim came as prosecutors alleged he exploited two 14-year-old girls who were too intoxicated to consent.

Monsoon to advance deeper, but this state will still face heatwave on SaturdayThe IMD forecasts heavy to very heavy rainfall across eastern, northeastern and southern India on June 27, while severe heatwave conditions continue over parts of Uttar Pradesh as the southwest monsoon advances.

200 kg silver bricks stolen from Ayodhya's Ram Mandir? SIT probe's big revealThe SIT, probing the Ram Mandir donation theft case, said silver bars and bricks donated to the temple were not stolen. Its probe found most were melted or sent for minting.

Kangana Ranaut says families should not be judged in Pune fort murder caseActor Kangana Ranaut has voiced that families should not be blamed for the actions of their children in the Pune fort murder case, emphasising the influence of social media, AI, and peer pressure.

Toll from twin Venezuela earthquakes climbs to 589, nearly 3,000 injuredForeign rescue teams – including some from countries which have opposed Venezuela during decades of international isolation, political repression and economic deterioration – began arriving late on Thursday, with a small contingent from the Dominican Republic the first to reach La Guaira.

Pak digs its Indic roots for Indus waters, gets some Aussie, British helpBritish and Australian envoys have recently highlighted Pakistan's heritage through funding and a Taxila visit. The timing is suspicious. Islamabad has been increasingly invoking the Indus Valley Civilisation during the Indus waters dispute with India.

ED seizes Rs 3.66 crore life insurance policies in Rs 1,200-crore SBI fraud caseThe ED has attached two Zurich International Life insurance policies linked to Shrikant Bhasi in the AOPL case. The move follows findings that the policies were funded through connected overseas entities and were being readied for surrender.

sports.headline: Sri Lanka vs Scotland: Live Score and Updates from Manchester SL-W vs SCO-W, Women's T20 World Cup Live Updates: Sri Lanka have won the toss and opted to bowl first at Old Trafford in Manchester. Chamari Athapaththu's troops are looking to stay alive in the competition.

After loss in captaincy debut, Shreyas Iyer fires a warning to Team India IRE vs IND: Ireland beat India by 34 runs in Belfast, their first international win over them. Shreyas Iyer warned his side against complacency as the series now hangs on Sunday's final T20I.

Ireland ruin Shreyas Iyer's captaincy debut to seal historic T20I win IRE vs IND, 1st T20I: Lorcan Tucker's Ireland stunned Shreyas Iyer's India on Friday, beating the reigning T20 world champions by 34 runs in Belfast. Chasing 183 for victory, India were bowled out for 148, as Ireland registered their first-ever win over India in international cricket.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi omission vs Ireland sparks social media frenzy Shreyas Iyer confirmed Vaibhav Sooryavanshi will miss India's first T20I against Ireland in Belfast. The call has sharpened the debate between rewarding form and backing an established core.

Who is Rounak Chouhan? 18-year-old from Chhattisgarh stuns Chou Tien Chen at US Open US Open 2026: 18-year-old Rounak Chouhan upset top seed Chou Tien Chen in straight games at the US Open. The win marked his first top-10 scalp and underlined his rise from India's junior ranks.

Alexander Isak scolds Anthony Elanga over knockout qualification frustration FIFA World Cup 2026: Anthony Elanga scored in Sweden's 1-1 draw with Japan before thinking the result had knocked them out. The mix-up quickly gave way to relief as Sweden advanced among the best third-placed teams.

Why India delayed Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's debut in Ireland India left 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi out of the first T20I against Ireland in Belfast. Shreyas Iyer said senior World Cup winners were backed, a call Sanjay Manjrekar hailed as merit-based.

Who will Serena Williams face on her Slam comeback? Wimbledon draw explained Wimbledon 2026: Serena Williams will begin her campaign against Australia's rising star Maya Joint after receiving a wildcard for her first Grand Slam singles appearance since the 2022 US Open.

Wimbledon draw: Novak Djokovic in Sinner's half, faces tough road to quarters Wimbledon 2026: Novak Djokovic has landed in Jannik Sinner's half of the draw. Carlos Alcaraz's withdrawal opens the field, but a demanding route still shapes Djokovic's bid for a 25th Grand Slam title.

1st T20I Highlights: Iyer's captaincy debut ends in vain as Ireland scripts history India (IND) vs Ireland (IRE) Highlights, 1st T20I: IND (148/10 in 18.5 ov), chasing 183. Shreyas Iyer's captaincy debut ended in vain as Ireland scripted history by winning their first ever T20I against India.

Ecuador coach's euphoric celebration after mega-upset vs Germany goes viralEcuador coach Sebastian Beccacece's emotional celebration after his side's dramatic 2-1 win over Germany went viral as the South American nation reached the FIFA World Cup knockout stage for the first time since 2006. Beccacece sprinted into the stands to embrace his wife and supporters after Ecuador completed a remarkable turnaround.

Germany camp divided? Undav, Kimmich contradict Nagelsmann after Ecuador defeatGermany manager Julian Nagelsmann dismissed suggestions of a lack of desire as "nonsense", but captain Joshua Kimmich and striker Deniz Undav painted a very different picture after their side's 2-1 defeat to Ecuador in their final Group E match at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

Let's play like Lions: Shreyas Iyer delivers first huddle talk as T20I captainShreyas Iyer addressed India's huddle in Belfast before his first T20I as captain. His message centred on culture, support and creating a fun team environment as a new leadership phase begins.

Bengal T20 League is creating the next IPL stars: Shahbaz AhmedThe Bengal T20 League has emerged as a key platform for young cricketers looking to break into the IPL and Bengal's senior teams. Speaking to India Today, Rarh Tigers captain Shahbaz Ahmed urged youngsters to focus on performances rather than IPL contracts, saying selectors and scouts are already watching the tournament closely.

Was 16-year-old Sachin as viral as Sooryavanshi before his India debut?Long before algorithms and reels, a 16-year-old Sachin Tendulkar captivated India without a single smartphone in sight. As Vaibhav Sooryavanshi debuts, we revisit an analogue era when a nation fell for a myth built on whispers.

Barely past the group stage, FIFA World Cup 2026 rewrites goalscoring recordsThe FIFA World Cup 2026 has already entered the record books. Barely past the group stage, the expanded tournament became the highest-scoring edition in history. Lionel Messi leads the goalscoring charts at the moment with 5 strikes to his name.

Australia advance after Paraguay draw as USA top Group D despite Turkey defeatFIFA World Cup: Australia booked their place in the Round of 32 after holding Paraguay to a hard-fought 0-0 draw in their final Group D match on Thursday, while the United States finished top of the group despite suffering a dramatic 3-2 defeat to Turkey in Los Angeles.

Anushka Yadav's heavy metal miracle at The KalingaAt the Kalinga Stadium, 18-year-old Anushka Yadav broke the women's hammer throw national record twice in one night, throwing 67.02m to qualify for the 2026 Asian Games. She announced herself as one of India's brightest field-event prospects.

Man City agree club-record deal with Forest to sign World Cup star Elliot AndersonManchester City have agreed a club-record deal worth £116 million with Nottingham Forest to sign England midfielder Elliot Anderson, with the 23-year-old set to undergo a medical before completing his move to the Etihad Stadium.

National holiday declared in Ecuador after country qualifies for WC knockoutsEcuador declared a national holiday after their dramatic 2-1 comeback win over Germany which sealed them a place in the FIFA World Cup knockout rounds for the first time in 20 years. President Daniel Noboa hailed the team's resilience in a difficult campaign.

Netherlands top Group F as Japan and Sweden reach knockoutsThe Netherlands sealed top spot in Group F with a 3-1 win over Tunisia, while Japan booked their place in the last 32 after a 1-1 draw with Sweden. Sweden also progressed as one of the best third-placed teams, setting up knockout ties for both the Dutch and Japan.

Sooryavanshi hype overshadows Shreyas Iyer's debut as India captain in BelfastIndia begin a new T20I era under captain Shreyas Iyer against Ireland in Belfast, with attention split between leadership transition and 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's potential debut. Ireland, led by Lorcan Tucker, also eye opportunities for young players as both teams deal with transitions and challenging playing conditions.

Australia reach knockouts, USA top Group D despite loss to TurkeyAustralia (AUS) vs Paraguay (PAR) highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: USA and Australia qualify for the Round of 32 of the FIFA World Cup. Australia held Paraguay to a 0-0 draw, while the USA lost to Turkey 2-3 in a last-gasp goal by Kaan Ayhan. Despite the loss, USA top Group D.

Iran seek knockout berth as coach Amir Ghalenoei avoids Pride celebration debateIran aim to make their nation proud against Egypt with a place in the World Cup knockout stages on the line, coach Amir Ghalenoei said on Thursday, while refusing to be drawn into the debate over the Pride celebrations surrounding the match.

FIFA World Cup 2026 shatters attendance record despite US travel messThe 2026 FIFA World Cup has become the most-attended edition in history, crossing 3.6 million spectators with nearly half the tournament still to play. The milestone arrives despite criticism over expensive tickets, visa hurdles and logistical concerns across the United States.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's India debut faces rain threat in Belfast? Here's the weather forecastIND vs IRE, 1st T20I: Rain could threaten India's first T20I against Ireland in Belfast, with weather forecasts predicting intermittent showers. The weather may also delay Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's much-anticipated India debut.

TUN 1-3 NED Highlights: Dutch finish top of Group F, Japan and Sweden go throughNetherlands (NED) vs Tunisia (TUN), FIFA World Cup Highlights: (TUN 1-3 NED) The Netherlands topped Group F with a 3-1 win over Tunisia, while Japan secured second place after a 1-1 draw with Sweden. Sweden also progressed as one of the tournament's best third-placed teams.

Ecuador script stunning Germany upset to keep World Cup dream aliveFIFA World Cup 2026: Ecuador came from behind to produce one of the biggest shocks of the 2026 World Cup, stunning Germany 2-1 in New Jersey. The comeback secured Sebastián Beccacece's side a place in the knockout stages as one of the tournament's best third-placed teams.

Nicolas Pepe's brace closes curtain on Curacao, creates history for Ivory CoastNicolas Pepe struck twice as Ivory Coast beat Curacao 2-0 to reach the FIFA World Cup knockout stage for the first time. Curacao bowed out after an inspiring debut campaign despite a spirited display.

FIFA World Cup 2026: Seattle's Pride Match puts Egypt vs Iran clash in spotlightFIFA said fans can carry rainbow flags into the Egypt-Iran World Cup match in Seattle. The decision has sharpened attention on Pride celebrations opposed by both teams' federations.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
